articles.title: Dollar Rallies on Crude Oil Strength as Hot US CPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) today is up by +0.45%. The dollar is climbing today on renewed concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down after President Trump said the current ceasefire was on "life support." Today's +3% surge in crude oil prices also boosts inflation expectations and may prompt...

articles.title: Pound Sterling slipped after hot US CPI with PPI still ahead
articles.expand: GBP/USD lost about 0.7% on Tuesday, sliding from prior session highs near 1.3650 to test the 1.3500 round figure before a modest late-session rebound.

articles.title: Hot US inflation print fans fears of Fed rate hike as energy costs spread
articles.expand: US consumer prices rose 3.8% in April, above forecasts, with energy accounting for 40% of the increase, prompting analysts to warn of rising rate hike risks and pushing December hike odds to nearly 30%. Summary: The US Consumer Price Index rose 3.8% year-on-year in April, ahead of the 3.7% consensus forecast and up from 3.3% in March, with energy p...

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Bulls move in as pair bounces toward 158.00
articles.expand: USD/JPY recovers some ground and rallies towards a daily four-day high near 157.80 as traders digest comments from US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on undesirable volatility in the FX space, prompting a Yen buy. At the time of writing, the pair is up by over 0.30%.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar eased after hot US CPI with RBNZ inflation survey next on the slate
articles.expand: NZD/USD edged lower in choppy two-way trade on Tuesday, settling slightly down for the session. The pair set its peak during the Asian and early European hours, then fell to a session low around the 0.5935 area mid-session, before recovering part of the move into the New York close.

articles.title: Australian Dollar pared post-CPI losses as Chalmers budget answered the oil shock
articles.expand: AUD/USD swung in a choppy intraday range on Tuesday before settling little changed for the session.

articles.title: Gold (XAU/USD) rises slow and steady – In-depth Gold technical analysis
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) update: Gold (XAU/USD) remains remarkably resilient, consolidating within a wide $4,500 to $4,900 range despite a hot US CPI print and a surging Dollar. As the metals complex digests China’s 45-month high inflation and pivots toward the high-stakes Trump-Xi summit, explore our in-depth technical analysis for Gold’s next major move.

articles.title: Forex Today: Hot US CPI boosts US Dollar as Treasury yields climb
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) rallied toward the 98.30 region on Tuesday as hotter-than-expected United States (US) inflation data boosted Treasury yields and reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may keep interest rates elevated for longer.

articles.title: ECB's Nagel: We still have quite a bit of inflation ahead of us
articles.expand: Joachim Nagel, President of the Bundesbank and member of the European Central Bank (ECB), told Handelsblatt on Tuesday that ECB rate hikes are becoming increasingly likely with the high inflation that is ahead of them.

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Don't think Fed independence is going away
articles.expand: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Chicago, said that without central bank independence, inflation will come roaring back at the Greater Rockford Chamber of Commerce in Rockford, Illinois on Tuesday.

articles.title: US Dollar Index jumps after hot US CPI data reinforces hawkish Fed outlook
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback’s value against a basket of six major currencies, climbs to a five-day high on Tuesday as stronger-than-expected US inflation data bolsters the hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) outlook.

articles.title: Wall Street Poised For Lower Open Geopolitical Situation Dampens Investor Appetite
articles.expand: (RTTNews) - Initial trends from the U.S. Futures Index suggest that Wall Street might open lower on Tuesday. Investors are worried about the rising oil prices and inflation. Geopolitical situation remains turbulent with doubts over the U.S.-Iran ceasefire. There are reports that the Trump administration may consider military action against Iran.

articles.title: Sudan: Central Bank Continues Field Tours in Omdurman to Monitor Currency Replacement
articles.expand: [SNA] The Operations and Follow-up Chamber for Currency Replacement at the Central Bank of Sudan (CBOS) continued on Monday its field inspection tours of several commercial bank branches in Omdurman to monitor the replacement process for the old 1,000- and 500-pound banknotes.

articles.expand: Disney’s annual design budget for just one of its six theme park resorts is equivalent to the price of the most advanced tank in the U.S. army according to recently released documents.
